summary: Email Delivery Feature Complete - All 14 backend tests pass (100%), all frontend features verified. Resend integration works correctly with sandbox mode gracefully handling non-verified emails.
critical_code_review_comments: email.py: Properly handles Resend API errors and returns structured response {success, email_id, error}
invites.py: _send_and_track atomically updates delivery status after send attempt
invites.py: resend endpoint correctly validates invite status before resending (400 for non-pending)
InvitesPage.js: DeliveryBadge component properly shows sent/failed/pending with tooltips for errors
InvitesPage.js: Toast notifications differentiate between successful send vs failed (fallback copy-link)
InvitesPage.js: Resend count displayed when > 0 with spinner during resend
seed_data_creation: Multiple test invites created during pytest (test_* prefixed emails)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Email delivery feature fully working. Resend is in sandbox mode so emails to non-verified addresses fail gracefully with delivery_status='failed'. douglas@capymatch.com is the only verified email in sandbox mode. All delivery tracking fields (delivery_status, sent_at, last_error, resend_count) properly stored and displayed.

backend_email_delivery: POST /api/invites creates invite AND attempts email send, returns delivery_status field
POST /api/invites/{id}/resend resends email and increments resend_count
Invite response includes: delivery_status, sent_at, last_error, resend_count
GET /api/invites list includes delivery tracking fields for all invites
When email send fails, invite still exists with status=pending and delivery_status=failed
frontend_delivery_ui: Resend button visible for pending invites with spinner while resending
Toast shows success when email sent vs warning when email failed
Invite form closes after successful creation
frontend_rbac: Director sees Invite link in header
Coach does NOT see Invite link in header
Coach redirected to mission-control when accessing /invites directly
